jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer's car crash during opening credits
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase, perfectly integrated into his character. Economical and quotable.

jokes.text: Eye of the tiger, Bart. Eye of the-- [Grunts] - I stopped it. - And I hurt somebody. Ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Coach trying to inspire with 'Eye of the Tiger' reference
jokes.punchline: Nelson's gleeful admission that he hurt someone
jokes.explanation: Classic Nelson - his joy at causing pain is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Thanks, Dad!
jokes.setup: Coach announces Nelson won the camp spot
jokes.punchline: Nelson calls the coach 'Dad' revealing their relationship
jokes.explanation: Reveals nepotism in a single economical line - classic Simpsons institutional critique.

jokes.text: It's called Barton Fink.
jokes.setup: Kids wanting to sneak into R-rated movie
jokes.punchline: The 'forbidden' movie is an obscure art film
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectation - kids aren't sneaking into typical forbidden content but highbrow cinema.

jokes.text: Barton Fink! Barton Fink! Barton Fink!
jokes.setup: Previous joke about the art house movie
jokes.punchline: Kids chanting the name like it's exciting
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the previous joke, building the absurdity through repetition.

jokes.text: What'd you say, Marge?
jokes.setup: Marge clearly told him to pick up Bart before leaving
jokes.punchline: Homer wasn't listening and has no idea what she said
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - completely oblivious and setting up the episode's main conflict.

jokes.text: Tonight on Wings-- Ah, who cares?
jokes.setup: TV announcer promoting tonight's Wings episode
jokes.punchline: Even the announcer doesn't care about the show
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about TV quality - announcer breaking character is unexpectedly honest.

jokes.text: Homer Simpson. Homer Simpson. Pick up Bart. Pick up Bart!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to remember what he's supposed to do
jokes.punchline: His mnemonic device of repeating his own name first
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's simple mind - he has to remember his own name before the task.

jokes.text: Trab Pu Kcip. Trab Pu Kcip!
jokes.setup: Homer writing 'Pick up Bart' backwards on the wall
jokes.punchline: The backwards nonsense phrase he created
jokes.explanation: Absurd and memorable - Homer's backwards writing becoming a quotable nonsense phrase.

jokes.text: Bart. Bart. Bart, Bart, Bart, Bart. - Bart. - [Belching] Bart.
jokes.setup: TV crowd chanting 'Bart' triggers Homer's memory
jokes.punchline: Even Barney's belch sounds like 'Bart'
jokes.explanation: Escalating repetition with Barney's belch as perfect punchline - very economical.

jokes.text: Pick up Bart. Pick up Bart. 'Pigabar'? What the hell is 'pigabar'?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to decipher his own backwards writing
jokes.punchline: He can't figure out his own mnemonic device
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity - defeated by his own backwards writing system.

jokes.text: Why? How? When? Which?
jokes.setup: Homer discovering Bart is gone
jokes.punchline: His panicked rapid-fire questions including nonsensical 'Which?'
jokes.explanation: Escalating panic with 'Which?' being grammatically nonsensical in context.

jokes.text: Dad, hide your shame! - Hey, Homie! I can see your doodle. - Shutup, Flanders.
jokes.setup: Homer running around naked looking for Bart
jokes.punchline: Flanders' innocent observation about Homer's nudity
jokes.explanation: Classic Flanders innocence meets Homer's embarrassment - 'doodle' is perfectly childish.

jokes.text: We could sit here and try to figure out who forgot to pick up who till the cows come home. But let's just say we're both wrong, and that'll be that.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to apologize for forgetting Bart
jokes.punchline: His 'apology' that assigns equal blame to the child he abandoned
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - completely avoiding responsibility while seeming reasonable.

jokes.text: Well, actually the last half hour's a real garbage dump.
jokes.setup: Krusty promoting his show
jokes.punchline: Admitting half his show is terrible
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on TV quality with Krusty's characteristic honesty about his show.

jokes.text: I miss Joe Piscopo.
jokes.setup: Homer watching bad TV
jokes.punchline: Missing a widely mocked comedian
jokes.explanation: Perfect throwaway line - Joe Piscopo was widely considered unfunny, making Homer's nostalgia absurd.

jokes.text: My name is Bart Simpson and I don't have a father.
jokes.setup: Bart calling Bigger Brothers
jokes.punchline: Claiming to be orphaned when he's just mad at Homer
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - child lying about being orphaned to get attention, very Bart.

jokes.text: This goes on for 12 more minutes.
jokes.setup: Homer watching terrible TV show
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall to complain about runtime
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on bad TV with Homer as viewer surrogate - very economical.

jokes.text: Yeah. He left me out on the curb for the ashman. What a revoltin' development.
jokes.setup: Pepi describing his abandonment
jokes.punchline: Casual description of horrific abandonment with TV catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Dark humor mixed with old TV reference - child trauma delivered with sitcom catchphrase.

jokes.text: Marge, when kids these days say 'bad,' they mean 'good.'
jokes.setup: Homer defending himself against Bart calling him a bad father
jokes.punchline: Completely outdated understanding of slang
jokes.explanation: Homer's desperate rationalization using outdated slang knowledge.

jokes.text: And to shake your booty means to wiggle one's butt. Permit me to demonstrate. - No!
jokes.setup: Homer explaining more outdated slang
jokes.punchline: Offering to demonstrate butt wiggling
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy threat with Marge's horror - typical Homer inappropriate timing.

jokes.text: It's not fair. It's not fair, I tells ya.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining record club charges
jokes.punchline: Childish tantrum about obvious scam
jokes.explanation: Homer's childish reaction to being scammed by obvious mail-order trick.

jokes.text: Dad, can I-- - Yeah, yeah, yeah.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to ask permission
jokes.punchline: Homer agreeing without listening
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer inattentiveness - agreeing to things without listening, very economical.

jokes.text: All right, Mom. I promise you will never be billed for another call.
jokes.setup: Lisa promising to stop calling Corey hotline
jokes.punchline: Technically correct but misleading promise
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection - Lisa finding loophole in her promise, shows her intelligence.

jokes.text: Nineteen. - Hit me. - Twenty. - Hit me. - Twenty-one. - Hit me. - Twenty-two. - D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's dad gambling their life savings
jokes.punchline: Continuing to hit after 21, inevitable bust
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure - each 'hit me' more absurd, ending with signature 'D'oh!'

jokes.text: Boo! This is better than dart day.
jokes.setup: Communist Party recruiter being booed at baseball game
jokes.punchline: Homer enjoying the hostility more than usual entertainment
jokes.explanation: Homer finding entertainment in crowd hostility - reveals his simple pleasures.

jokes.text: Here's a good one-- 'Hawkeye's antics irritate the other surgeons.'
jokes.setup: Doctor giving Bart a MASH coloring book
jokes.punchline: Describing a coloring book page about character conflict
jokes.explanation: Absurd concept - coloring book about interpersonal workplace drama.

jokes.text: Here are some words that rhyme with Corey: Glory, story-- allegory, Montessori.
jokes.setup: Phone hotline providing rhymes for celebrity's name
jokes.punchline: Escalating to increasingly obscure/sophisticated rhymes
jokes.explanation: Escalation from simple to sophisticated rhymes - absurd hotline content.

jokes.text: That's not meatball soup. That's my collection of fur balls in stomach acid.
jokes.setup: Character complimenting the soup
jokes.punchline: Disgusting revelation about the 'soup'
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor typical of Ren & Stimpy being watched by the family.

jokes.text: Don't thank me. Thank an unprecedented eight-year military buildup.
jokes.setup: Bart using military weapon toy effectively
jokes.punchline: Crediting military-industrial complex for his success
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire delivered by 10-year-old - subversive and quotable.

jokes.text: Uh, I have a horsie. [Imitates Neighing]
jokes.setup: Show and tell after Bart's impressive military presentation
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's pathetic horse imitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse - completely outclassed and pathetically trying to participate.

jokes.text: Wuss!
jokes.setup: Milhouse's weak show and tell
jokes.punchline: Nelson's predictable mockery
jokes.explanation: One word perfectly captures Nelson's character - economical and in-character.

jokes.text: We'll show 'em all! [Laughing] [Snoring]
jokes.setup: Grampa getting excited about proving old people are fun
jokes.punchline: Immediately falling asleep mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa - enthusiastic declaration immediately undercut by reality of aging.

jokes.text: Hi, this is Corey. I hope you and I can get married someday.
jokes.setup: Lisa calling expensive celebrity hotline
jokes.punchline: Creepy romantic message to unknown callers
jokes.explanation: Creepy celebrity hotline content - inappropriate for young fans.

jokes.text: So long... science. Ah, music and art.
jokes.setup: Skinner cutting school budget
jokes.punchline: Casually eliminates essential subjects
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire about education budget cuts. Skinner's casual attitude toward eliminating subjects is darkly funny.

jokes.text: Good Lord! That's a 900 number.
jokes.setup: Skinner discovering phone making expensive calls
jokes.punchline: His shocked realization about premium numbers
jokes.explanation: Skinner's naive shock at discovering expensive phone lines - good character moment.

jokes.text: Canada stalls on trade pact.
jokes.setup: Expensive celebrity hotline
jokes.punchline: Reading boring news instead of celebrity content
jokes.explanation: Absurd - paying premium rates to hear mundane news about Canada.

jokes.text: Bart, I could kiss you if the Bigger Brothers hadn't made me sign a form promising I wouldn't.
jokes.setup: Tom expressing affection for Bart's thoughtfulness
jokes.punchline: Legal disclaimer preventing physical affection
jokes.explanation: Dark commentary on institutional safeguards - well-meaning mentorship hampered by bureaucracy.

jokes.text: How would you like me to take it? 'Go ahead, Bart. Have your fun. I'll be waiting for you'? I'm sorry. I can't do it!
jokes.setup: Homer confronting Bart about his new father figure
jokes.punchline: Dramatic breakdown over imagined patient waiting
jokes.explanation: Homer's jealous breakdown - can't handle being replaced, very dramatic and in-character.

jokes.text: Don't say revenge. Don't say revenge. Uh, revenge?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to think of good reason for little brother
jokes.punchline: Saying exactly what he told himself not to say
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity - internal monologue fails immediately. Classic structure.

jokes.text: Ugly. Wiener. Crater-face. Suey! Suey!
jokes.setup: Homer looking at potential little brothers
jokes.punchline: Cruelly judging children like livestock
jokes.explanation: Homer's cruelty toward children - calling them like pigs is particularly harsh.

jokes.text: I'll take him. Do you have him in blond?
jokes.setup: Homer finally choosing a little brother
jokes.punchline: Treating child selection like shopping for merchandise
jokes.explanation: Treating child adoption like retail shopping - darkly funny and economical.

jokes.text: Oh, there's Mother now-- watching me. What's that, Mother? They have a right to be here. It's school business. I-- Mother, that sailor suit doesn't fit anymore!
jokes.setup: Skinner explaining his relationship with his mother
jokes.punchline: Having imaginary conversation about his childish clothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner - dominated by mother, one-sided conversation reveals his infantilization.

jokes.text: Just like Oscar the Grouch.
jokes.setup: Homer seeing Pepi living in poverty
jokes.punchline: Comparing child's dire situation to Sesame Street character
jokes.explanation: Homer's inappropriate pop culture reference to child poverty - shows his insensitivity.

jokes.text: Your son Bart sounds very bad. Oh, he is.
jokes.setup: Pepi commenting on Bart's behavior
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately agreeing his son is bad
jokes.explanation: Homer readily agreeing his son is bad - shows his emotional distance from Bart.

jokes.text: Because it's a stupid piece of junk!
jokes.setup: Pepi asking why elevator stops
jokes.punchline: Homer's sudden rage at inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Homer's sudden anger at machinery - typical misdirected frustration.

jokes.text: Well, there's jerry the cowboy. And that big dipper looking thing is Alan... the cowboy.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to name constellations for Pepi
jokes.punchline: Making up cowboys for all constellations
jokes.explanation: Homer's ignorance masked as wisdom - making up constellation names poorly but confidently.

jokes.text: 'Learnd,' son. It's pronounced 'learnd.'
jokes.setup: Pepi calling Homer learned
jokes.punchline: Homer 'correcting' pronunciation incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - trying to sound smart while being completely wrong about pronunciation.

jokes.text: I love you, Papa Homer. I love you too, Pepsi. Pepi. Pepi.
jokes.setup: Touching moment between Homer and Pepi
jokes.punchline: Homer calling him by wrong name, then soft drink brand
jokes.explanation: Homer ruining tender moment with name confusion - 'Pepsi' is particularly absurd.

jokes.text: I made it.
jokes.setup: Lisa struggling with phone addiction all night
jokes.punchline: Celebrating making it to midnight by seconds
jokes.explanation: Lisa's triumph over addiction - barely making the deadline, very economical.

jokes.text: Who the hell is Pepi? He's my little brother. That's right. You're not the only one who can abuse a nonprofit organization.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about Pepi
jokes.punchline: Homer openly admitting to abusing charity system
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - Homer proudly admitting to charity fraud for petty revenge.

jokes.text: I was fakin' it. - Liar! - Remember this? 'Higher, Dad. Higher. Whee! Whee! Push harder, Dad.' - Hey, stop that.
jokes.setup: Bart claiming he faked enjoying time with Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer imitating Bart's childhood enthusiasm mockingly
jokes.explanation: Painful father-son fight - Homer's mock imitation of child Bart is cruel but funny.

jokes.text: You know, the whale is not really a fish. They're mammals, like you and me. Is that true? No.
jokes.setup: Tom sharing educational fact about whales
jokes.punchline: Bart's deadpan correction that it's false
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan - Bart casually correcting accurate information with complete confidence.

jokes.text: Hey! He got my hot dog!
jokes.setup: Homer fighting with dolphin
jokes.punchline: Indignant about dolphin taking his food
jokes.explanation: Homer's petty concern about food while fighting aquatic mammal - typical priorities.

jokes.text: His father-- the drunken gambler? That's right.
jokes.setup: Tom identifying Homer from Bart's description
jokes.punchline: Homer proudly confirming the unflattering description
jokes.explanation: Homer accepting the harsh but accurate description without shame - typical lack of self-awareness.

jokes.text: Early reports indicate one of the fighters is a giant lizard. Do we have a source on this? A bunch of drunken frat boys.
jokes.setup: News report about the fight
jokes.punchline: Unreliable witnesses providing absurd information
jokes.explanation: Media satire - news taking obviously unreliable drunk sources seriously.

jokes.text: This is even more painful than it looks.
jokes.setup: Homer in middle of brutal fight
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about his own suffering
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan delivery during violence - Homer's casual commentary on his own pain.

jokes.text: I already bought a giant ham for dinner. It's gonna go to waste. Don't talk about food. I'm so hungry.
jokes.setup: Three men with various problems after the fight
jokes.punchline: Homer's priority is still food waste
jokes.explanation: Even in crisis, Homer thinks about food - perfectly in character priorities.

jokes.text: Dad, remember when Tom had you in that headlock and you screamed, 'I'm a hemophiliac' and when he let you go you kicked him in the back?
jokes.setup: Bart asking Homer about the fight
jokes.punchline: Homer's cowardly but effective fighting technique
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cowardly fighting strategy - fake medical condition for sympathy then attack. Perfectly Homer.

jokes.text: First, you gotta shriek like a woman. And keep sobbing till he turns away in disgust. That's when it's time to kick some back.
jokes.setup: Homer teaching Bart his fighting technique
jokes.punchline: Detailed cowardly strategy involving fake crying
jokes.explanation: Homer teaching terrible life lessons - cowardly fighting as father-son bonding.

jokes.text: And then when he's lying out on the ground-- Yeah? Kick him in the ribs. Yeah? Step on his neck. Yeah? And run like hell.
jokes.setup: Homer continuing his fighting lesson
jokes.punchline: Escalating to brutal attacks then cowardly escape
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with Bart's enthusiastic 'Yeah?' responses - dark father-son bonding.
